---
title: "Analyses of SAM-ratings"
author: "Maximillian Helles"
format: html
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
source("_setup.R")
```

```{r}
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# PREPARE DATA: EXCLUDE CONTROL BLOCK
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Create a new dataset that only includes the real-stakes trials (low and high)
df_main_experiment <- df %>%
  filter(block_id != "control")

cat("\n(Dropped 'control' block. Remaining blocks:", paste(unique(df_main_experiment$block_id), collapse = ", "), ")\n")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ANALYSIS 1: RISK-SEEKING BEHAVIOR 
# (Directly tests H6: The House Money Effect vs. Break-Even)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Fit the Mixed-Effects Model for Risk-Seeking
model_risk_seeking <- glmer(
  is_risk_seeking ~ prev_condition + position + (1 | participant_id), 
  data = df_main_experiment, # <--- Using the filtered data here
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa") 
)

# 2. Check Overall Significance
cat("\n--- OVERALL ANOVA: RISK-SEEKING (No Control) ---\n")
Anova(model_risk_seeking, type = "II")

# 3. Check Specific Contrasts (The Hypothesis Test)
cat("\n--- PAIRWISE COMPARISONS: RISK-SEEKING (No Control) ---\n")
post_hoc_seeking <- emmeans(model_risk_seeking, pairwise ~ prev_condition, type = "response")
summary(post_hoc_seeking)

```

```{r}
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ANALYSIS 2: RISK-AVERSE BEHAVIOR 
# (Flight-to-safety)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Fit the Mixed-Effects Model for Risk Aversion
model_risk_aversion <- glmer(
  is_risk_averse ~ prev_condition + position + (1 | participant_id), 
  data = df_main_experiment, # <--- Using the filtered data here
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa") 
)

# 2. Check Overall Significance
cat("\n--- OVERALL ANOVA: RISK AVERSION (No Control) ---\n")
Anova(model_risk_aversion, type = "II")

# 3. Check Specific Contrasts
cat("\n--- PAIRWISE COMPARISONS: RISK AVERSION (No Control) ---\n")
post_hoc_aversion <- emmeans(model_risk_aversion, pairwise ~ prev_condition, type = "response")
summary(post_hoc_aversion)
```

```{r}
mean_t <- mean(df$action_index, na.rm = TRUE)
mean_jump_index <- mean(df$jump_index)

ggplot(df, aes(x = action_index)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_t, linetype = "dashed") + # Participant mean
  geom_vline(xintercept = 288 / 2, color = "red") + # Half of trial
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_jump_index, color = "green") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +
  labs(x = "Action Index", y = "Counts")

ggplot(df, aes(x = action_index*0.025)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "darkblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_t*0.025, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 288*0.025 / 2, color = "red") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_jump_index * 0.025, color = "green") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 288*0.025), breaks = 0:8) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +
  labs(x = "Action time (s)", y = "Counts")

```
